danger_signals.visual_form: Unit sprites with distinct enemy faction coloring approaching player structures
danger_signals.meaning: Active military threat requiring defensive response

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual degradation/smoking of buildings, reduced structural integrity display
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate vulnerability; base under attack or compromised

danger_signals.visual_form: Depleted or barren terrain where Tiberium crystals once grew
danger_signals.meaning: Economic threat; loss of funding capability and strategic resource denial

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy construction yards and production facilities visible on map
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent building military or economic advantage; escalating threat level

safety_signals.visual_form: Player-colored units (blue for GDI, red for Nod) within base perimeter
Harvester vehicles collecting Tiberium; crystal fields actively glowing
safety_signals.meaning: Economy stable and functioning; funding available for expansion

safety_signals.visual_form: Guard towers, walls, or defensive installations fully constructed and operational
safety_signals.meaning: Fortified position; capacity to resist attack

safety_signals.visual_form: MCV deployed and functioning; large construction facility visible
safety_signals.meaning: Ability to expand base and produce units; strategic viability maintained

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy GDI units appear in red or opposing faction colors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Orderly base layouts; geometric structure placement; reinforced military installations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Brotherhood symbol (curved line/flame motif); cult-like iconography
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy Nod units appear in blue or opposing faction colors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: More organic/irregular base layouts; occult/technological hybrid aesthetic; distinctive Nod architecture

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Crystalline Tiberium formations grow across terrain; infected areas display alien crystal growths in cyan/green
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Units operating in contaminated zones; harvester focus on Tiberium collection; strategic positioning around contamination points

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map reveals more enemy units and structures; explosions and fire effects increase; damaged terrain from combat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Both factions mobilize military units; increased construction activity; aggressive unit movement and attack patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Harvester units actively present; Tiberium fields glowing with available resources; construction yards actively building
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Steady unit production; expansion of base structures; research and technology upgrades available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy units surrounding perimeter; defensive structures actively engaged (firing animations); base structures smoking/damaged
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defensive units concentrated around threatened structures; emergency unit production; potential retreat or reinforcement behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UN-affiliated (GDI) structures display institutional design; Nod structures display revolutionary/cult aesthetic; contrast in architectural philosophy
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ideological faction behavior reflected in unit types and strategy emphasis; technology trees and unit rosters differ fundamentally

environmental_storytelling.example: The presence, density, and spread of Tiberium crystals across the map communicates the game's central conflict premise—contamination driving geopolitical war. Crystal fields indicate resource value and danger simultaneously.

GDI structures display military/governmental order (rectangular, reinforced); Nod structures display cult-revolutionary design (organic, imposing). Visual architecture alone communicates ideological difference without dialogue.

GDI units include infantry, tanks, helicopters reflecting conventional military doctrine; Nod units include stealth tanks, flame infantry, Temple of Nod reflecting unconventional/technological cult approach. Unit availability tells story of faction capabilities and philosophy.
Combat-scarred terrain, depleted Tiberium fields, and destroyed structures persist on the map, creating visual record of conflict history and current strategic value distribution.

Live-action cinematic storytelling with named commanders (Kane, General Shepherd, etc.) provides narrative context that maps to on-map faction dynamics. Character appearance and rhetoric reinforce faction ideology.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves directly to Tiberium fields, depletes crystals, returns to refinery; repeated cycles indicate economic stability
Remains stationary within base perimeter; indicates defensive readiness and garrison status
Movement toward target; continuous firing animations; persistent path toward enemy structure or unit indicates active threat engagement
Emits construction animation; new buildings appear nearby; indicates faction establishing/expanding territorial control
Turret rotates toward threat; firing animations activate; indicates active threat detection and deterrence
FMV cutscene appearance correlates with mission objective shifts; visual appearance and dialogue establish faction authority and ideological position

notes: Command & Conquer (1995) establishes visual communication patterns that became RTS genre standards. The Tiberium contamination serves dual function as both resource and existential threat, creating inherent visual-strategic alignment. Faction identity is communicated through color coding, architectural design philosophy, and unit roster diversity rather than explicit UI labels. The game's sidebar navigation and real-time unit production create continuous visual feedback about economic and military state. The use of full-motion video cutscenes with named commanders (Kane, General Shepherd) provides narrative authority that maps to on-screen faction dynamics, creating cognitive link between story context and strategic gameplay.
